draft: Write out the fat table's functional dependencies on paper first, then walk 1NF → 2NF → 3NF the way the exam expects. The graded fields are just reading off that work. The composite key of the line-item table is (order_id, book_title).

fields.label: Inkwell's spreadsheet is one fat row per line item, already atomic (1NF). Its key is the composite (order_id, book_title). What is the FIRST normal form it violates?
Which non-key columns depend on only PART of the composite key (order_id alone, or book_title alone) — the partial dependencies that break 2NF?
List the functional dependencies hiding in the fat table — the ones that make each order, customer, and book fact repeat on every line.
fields.help: One per line, as  a -> b  (a composite left side is  a, b -> c).

fields.label: After normalizing all the way to 3NF, which table does each fact live in? Drop each descriptive column into its home table.
fields.help: Keys and foreign keys are already placed; these are the descriptive facts.
